title: No Further H5 Bird Flu Detections in NZ, MPI Confirms
description_clean: The Ministry for Primary Industries (MPI) says no new cases of H5 bird flu have been detected following a case found earlier this week. MPI's chief veterinary officer, Dr Mary van Andel, says the Ministry thanks the public for their vigilance. She says specialist investigators have been out at Petone beach and have found nothing of further concern. " It is not unusual at this time of year to find individual dead birds of different breeds along our coastlines, due to storms and other natural events," van Andel says. To date, there has been one single coastal detection in an individual ocean-goi
summary: MPI confirmed no new cases of H5 bird flu have been detected following an earlier single coastal detection in an ocean-going seabird, with investigators finding nothing of further concern at Petone beach.

title: Newly arrived H5N1 bird flu likely to take hold in New Zealand, MPI says
description_clean: A seabird found on Wellington's Petone beach has tested positive for the potentially deadly H5 strain of the virus.
summary: MPI says newly arrived H5N1 bird flu is likely to take hold in New Zealand after a seabird found on Wellington's Petone beach tested positive for the H5 strain.

title: 185 Beef Farms Sign Up as Halter's Satellite Fencing Delivers Early Gains
description_clean: Two months after unveiling a major upgrade to its beef product, Halter says its farmers are on track for major production gains and additional grass growth. Halter's Beef Pro was rolled out in May alongside a move to connect its smart collars directly to satellite in partnership with OneNZ and powered by Starlink. Since then, in New Zealand alone, 500 beef farms have joined Halter's satellite solution, with 185 farms on the new beef product. Halter's country director for New Zealand, Josh Townsend, says beef farmers are rapidly moving beyond virtual fencing to take full advantage of pasture an
summary: Two months after upgrading its beef product with satellite-connected smart collars via OneNZ and Starlink, Halter reports 500 NZ beef farms have joined its satellite solution, with 185 on the new beef product, and expects major production and grass-growth gains.

title: Scientists warn ‘carbon tunnel vision’ risks misguided climate policies on livestock
description_clean: “Carbon tunnel vision” that over emphasises greenhouse gas emissions as the primary metric evaluating environmental impacts risks compromising reasonable pathways to reducing livestock emissions, a new scientific paper warns.
summary: A new scientific paper warns that 'carbon tunnel vision', over-emphasising greenhouse gas emissions as the primary environmental metric, risks compromising reasonable pathways to reducing livestock emissions.

title: First H5 Bird Flu Case Confirmed in NZ
description_clean: New Zealanders are being urged to be alert following a confirmed positive case of H5 bird flu this week. Biosecurity Minister Andrew Hoggard says the case, a brown skua, was found on Petone beach in Wellington. H5 bird flu is a low health risk to the public as it rarely affects humans unless there is direct, close, and prolonged contact with large numbers of sick birds. Eggs and poultry remain safe to eat. Hoggard says the case is a coastal detection in an individual ocean-going seabird and there is no evidence of any mass mortality wildlife or transmission between wild birds in New Zealand. "
summary: New Zealand confirmed its first H5 bird flu case, a brown skua found on Petone beach in Wellington, with Biosecurity Minister Andrew Hoggard noting a low public health risk and no evidence of mass mortality or wild bird transmission.

title: Zoetis pushes past $1 million in Beyond Blue mental health charity support, as 2026 campaign starts
description_clean: Animal health company Zoetis has partnered with the Beyond Blue mental health support charity since 2016, donating more than $1 million over that period to help fund support services for people living outside major cities. This year marks the 11th year of the partnership, with Zoetis aiming to raise another $100,000 by donating $5 from each sale of the company’s livestock and companion animal vaccines and parasiticide products from now until the end of October
summary: Animal health company Zoetis has donated more than $1 million to Beyond Blue since 2016 and, in its 11th year of partnership, aims to raise another $100,000 by donating $5 from each sale of select products until the end of October.
